title: Brand DNA
description: Voice, tone rules, CTAs, value proposition, and content guardrails injected into all Claude content generation prompts.

voice.description: Brand voice definition — how the brand sounds across all content.

adjectives.description: 3–6 words that describe the brand's personality.

style_statement.description: One sentence that defines the brand's writing style. Used verbatim in Claude prompts.
examples: Write like a senior consultant explaining strategy to a smart business owner — confident, clear, no jargon, always tied to outcomes.

reading_level.description: Target reading level for generated content.

tone_rules.description: Explicit do/do-not writing rules. Injected as a bullet list into the Claude system prompt.

rule.description: The tone rule as written in the prompt.

type.description: Whether this is a positive instruction or a prohibition.

examples.rule: Lead with the specific outcome before explaining the process
Use passive voice — always assign agency
Name frameworks and methodologies with proper nouns
Hedge claims with 'might', 'could', 'may' unless genuinely uncertain

value_proposition.description: Core value prop used in CTAs, hero text, and closing arguments.

examples: We build websites that rank, convert, and grow.

sub.description: Supporting sentence (under 120 chars). Backs up the headline.
examples: Full-stack digital growth for businesses that are serious about results.

differentiators.description: 2–4 bullet-point differentiators. Used in schema markup and comparison sections.

primary_cta.description: Default call-to-action injected at the end of every article.

url.description: Destination URL. Can be absolute or root-relative.

context_sentence.description: The sentence immediately before the CTA button. Should create desire and reduce friction.
examples: Ready to turn your website into your best-performing sales rep?

secondary_ctas.description: Alternative CTAs for mid-article placement or service-specific pages.

context.description: When to use this CTA (e.g., 'after SEO sections', 'on local pages').

forbidden_phrases.description: Words and phrases Claude must never use. Checked by AEO validator.

required_phrases.description: Phrases or named frameworks that must appear in every article (e.g., brand-owned methodology names).

content_disclaimers.description: Legal or editorial disclaimers to append to specific content types.

applies_to.description: Content type or topic this disclaimer applies to.
